text:
Agree - there is a tendency to ignore detail and find the common patterns. This is powerful though - and behind the general idea of 'finding abstractions' as well?

My feeling about universal AST is that it's a starting point but not a stopping point. What kinds of programs would be excluded by any chosen universal AST? Do we just end up with a 'general purpose graph/tree' structure (~infra) when we accommodate all the different styles? 

How does this scale up to multiple trees? Do we still use disjoint AST/trees or do we allow system visible cross links?

Also, what kinds of paradigms are excluded by this notion of programming? This is the batch model - i.e. must write in full and then submit/install your program to the system for execution.
